Sources

Knowledge sources, references, and publications

65 records

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Vistula River Valley (Poland)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Thermenlinie Wiener Becken (Austria)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Water Management Programme- Tarun Bharat Sangh

Case study detailing Tarun Bharat Sangh's johad-driven water revival and community governance model.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Stroomgebied Zuid Veluwe (Netherlands)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Strengthening Southeast Alaska’s Businesses — Together

Feature on Spruce Root’s Native CDFI services delivering flexible capital, coaching, and seasonal-fit lending.

Resilience Circles helps Indigenous Entrepreneurs to Scale Their Business Up

Article on Alaska Native Resilience Circles blending business training, wellness, and cultural leadership.

Bioregional Financing Facilities Book

This book introduces Bioregional Financing Facilities (BFFs)—institutional models that channel capital to regenerative initiatives, aligning finance with living systems and Indigenous wisdom.

r3.0 Bioregional Open Dialogue 3 - South Asia Bioregional Working Group

This Open Dialogue features the South Asia Bioregionalism Working Group, exploring bioregional visions, mapping, and planning across India and Bhutan.

Salmon Returns: A Biocultural Finance Collaborative

Booklet presenting Salmon Returns' biocultural finance model channeling capital into salmon-led regeneration.

Episode 12: Salmon Nation: Bioregional Scale Regeneration

Dialogue exploring Salmon Nation's bioregional vision, Indigenous partnerships, and regenerative finance pathways.

Salmon Biocultural Credit Flyer

Flyer introducing Salmon Nation's community-governed biocultural credit for watershed regeneration finance.

BioFi Field Building Call: Cascadia Across Scales

In this call, Brandon Letsinger and Brandy Gallagher share bioregional organizing efforts in Cascadia including the emerging BFF initiative.

Regenerate Cascadia 2025-2026 Landscape Hub Cultivator Pilot

Announcement outlining the Cascadia landscape hub pilot to co-create funding-ready regeneration teams.

Te Mahi Tiaki Taiao Report

Evaluation of Te Kete Hononga service supporting Hokianga taiao groups through kaupapa Māori restoration.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Oltenia de sub Munte (Romania)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

BioFi Field Building Call: Forests of the Northeast

In this call, the Northeast Bioregioning Collective share their journey of bioregional organizing, exploring Indigenous justice, and different ways of knowing.

Restoring land and seascapes in a tourist hotspot

Profile of Mallorca's alliance-driven effort to regenerate land and sea under tourism and climate pressures.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Laconia (Greece)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

El lewfu que volverá a correr

Mapuche community narrative on Afunalhue's changing waterways, biodiversity, and cultural resilience.

How LENs Works

Landscape Enterprise Networks (LENs) facilitate investment in nature-based and agricultural measures to make local landscapes healthier, more productive and resilient.

Developing a DAO for Regenerative Landscape Governance in Ecuador’s Chocó-Andean Biosphere Reserve

Comprehensive roadmap for establishing Guayabillas DAO to coordinate regenerative stewardship in the Chocó-Andean biosphere.

Bioregional Learning

Explainer on GTB Bioregional Learning Center anchoring regenerative education and systems coherence.

Bioregional Funding

Overview of the GTB bioregional funding ecosystem mobilizing aligned capital for regenerative portfolios.

BioFi Field Building Call: Bioregional Venture Studios

In this call, Greg Judelman and Cheryl Chen discuss building bioregional venture studios in the Greater Tkaronto Bioregion and Salmon Nation.

7-Generation GTB Circles of Activity

Overview of 7-Generation GTB circles connecting cohorts for regenerative action across the Greater Tkaronto bioregion.

The SPaCES Project

Mapping initiative supporting Forever Sabah with mangrove conservation planning and spatial services.

Sabah Renewable Energy Rural Electrification Roadmap

Consortium roadmap scaling renewable mini-grids for rural Sabah energy equity and climate resilience.

Riparian conservation and livelihoods on the Segama river

Initiative linking Segama communities and stakeholders to restore riparian biodiversity and resilient livelihoods.

Payment for Ecosystem Services (PES) Pilot Project

Pilot water fund aligning Babagon communities, government, and funders through equitable PES design.

Legal Innovation Team

Initiative crafting legal pathways for Sabah's ecological transition with a multimedia policy handbook.

Advancing Human-Elephant Harmony in telupid

Project profile on Telupid's community elephant rangers fostering coexistence with returning herds.

Forever Sabah Institute

Overview of Forever Sabah's learning hub nurturing changemakers with experiential, Indigenous-rooted education.

Nature-Positive Economy

Findhorn Watershed Initiative on a nature-positive rural economy: green jobs, blended public and philanthropic nature finance, aligned with Scotland’s just transition and catchment-scale stewardship.

Projects

Overview of Diamante Bridge Collective projects: land trusts, food forests, recycling, learning and arts commons, with Regen Finance pathways via Giveth, SDG Impact Fund, and Endaoment.

BioFi Field Building Call: Osa Peninsula

In this call, Tricia Stapleton of RegenerOsa and Doña Hazel Leitón of CEDPO share their regenerative work in Costa Rica's Osa Peninsula.

Growing a Prosperous Green Future Together - Strategic Plan 2024-2028

Five-year strategy outlining Carolinian Canada's Indigenous-centered plan for resilient, biodiverse communities.

The Deshkan Ziibi Conservation Impact Bond Project

Overview of an Indigenous-led conservation impact bond restoring Carolinian Canada through shared stewardship.

Food Economy

Catalyzing a Self-Sustaining Local Food Economy - Building resilience through local family farms, strengthened supply chains, and regenerative investment in Western North Carolina's foodshed

BioFi Cultivator Presentations - Day 1

Through their Cultivator Program, the BioFi Project guided teams to design and launch Bioregional Financing Facilities, which they present here.

Stocktake and Gap Analysis

Report summarizing Bioregioning Tayside's progress and gaps across 15 elements toward financing facilities.

Bioregional Finance

Bioregioning South East Ireland on bioregional finance: first Bioregional Basic Income pilot, integrated 4 Returns portfolios, catalyst grants, and emerging mechanisms for place-based investment.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Bioregional Weaving Alliance Ireland

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Celebrating the launch of the Bioregional Learning Alliance

Launch of the Bioregional Learning Alliance and Bioregioning in Practice webinar series—four years from summit conversations to a co-created learning programme with global bioregional practitioners.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 7

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 6

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 1

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

Bioregional Governance - European Case Studies & Framework

This study compiles bioregioning case studies and frameworks from Europe, highlighting shared principles and practices for regenerative governance.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 4

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

Bioregional Governance - Asian Case Studies & Framework

This study compiles bioregioning case studies and frameworks from Asia-Pacific, highlighting shared principles and practices for regenerative governance.

r3.0 Bioregional Open Dialogue 4 - Bioregioning in Australia

This Open Dialogue explores bioregioning in Australia through the lenses of Earth law, Indigenous relational governance, and local-scale action.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 3

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 2

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

Indigenous-Led Group Launches Cutting-Edge Biocultural Jaguar Credits

Article on Achuar-led jaguar credits blending blockchain finance with indigenous forest stewardship.

Cuencas Sagradas Bioregional Plan

Comprehensive roadmap weaving indigenous leadership and global action to protect the Amazon Sacred Headwaters.

The Bioregional Conversations - Conversation 5

The Bioregional Conversations brought together leading practitioners in a seven-part action-learning series exploring how bioregions are emerging and evolving around the world.

Territorios Regenerativos La Red

Iberian network connecting people, territories, and organizations toward landscape restoration—mission to 2030 (~16 territories, ~6M ha), collaboration with Commonland, Savory, Ashoka, and allied fram

Certificación Ecoregenerativa

Alvelal’s certification validates regenerative management of soil, water, and biodiversity for organic farms and supply chains seeking differentiation beyond conventional organic labels.

Bioregional Weaving Lab - Altiplano Estepario (Spain)

The Bioregional Weaving Lab, a network of changemakers regenerating landscapes, created this series of inspiring films showcasing diverse cultures and regenerative practices across Europe.

Reversing desertification with regenerative practices

Commonland's Altiplano project focuses on reversing desertification and erosion, restoring prosperity with the 4 Returns framework.

Saving Scotland's Rainforest with Natural Capital Investment

FIRNS pilot led by ACT for ASR: private natural capital for Scotland’s rainforest—standards, integrity charter, and woodland carbon model to help close the restoration funding gap.

The Common Ground Forum wins a Nature of Scotland Award

Blog celebrating the Common Ground Forum's innovation award for collaborative upland deer management.

Saving Scotland’s Rainforest with Natural Capital Finance

Report detailing Alliance for Scotland's Rainforest efforts to finance large-scale temperate rainforest restoration.

Saving Scotland's Rainforest

Progress update on efforts to restore and expand Scotland's temperate rainforest by 2045.

BioFi Field Building Call: Regenerating the Argentinian Andes

In this call, Acción Serrana and Un Árbol Más present their work regenerating the Argentinian Andes.

Through their Cultivator Program, the BioFi Project guided teams to design and launch Bioregional Financing Facilities, which they present here.
